item.title: It's time for yet another unhinged XL Thursday CeleryDump for the good & bad people of Imugur
item.description:
greetings online friends
one of my greatest fears
banish from household
ok
not really a banana fan tbh. i do like frozen ones covered in chocolate though
unhinged
whales, dinosaurs.. the possibilities are endless
what if we kissed in there
this explains so many things about my life
mmm hmm
this hit me hard
something fishy going on here..
octopus, any more brain busters?
not sure what i'd be doing if those all went away
i guess
real person + real bird.. it's real
my apologies for this
that's my time.. have a great night gang!!
item.dc:creator: CeleryMan7

item.description: (originally posted by @Suboftheday on 2023-11-23 01:11:24)
item.dc:creator: ThisTimeLastYear

item.title: Home goods makes the home good.
item.description: My wife and I were shopping at home goods for random stuff and came upon a glass soap dispenser. It's green with a gold top and a nice little pump for the soap. She said she hated it, and I put it back. A few days later I returned to that store to get the rest of the set of these glass ware things for our bathroom theme and the soap dispenser was still there. I bought the soap dispenser. She got mad at me for purchasing it, saying she didn't like the gold, and that it would clash with the bathroom theme (it doesn't). So she punished me, spanked me, call me a bad girl, and even broke out the riding crop. I came like 8 times. 5 stars, would purchase again.
item.dc:creator: WitchIrkalla

item.dc:creator: MayMyEnemiesLiveLong

item.title: 4 years worth of transitioning. Sometimes I still can't believe I get to be ME, slight political content
item.description: 4 years. 4 years of telling myself it's ok to now. 4 years of my partner hyping me up that it's worth trying. 4 years of surrounding myself with a great support net of friends. And now thanks to recent political events I will be leaving the country I was born in, the country I served, the country my children were born in, all because the new party thinks of me as trash. That somehow I'm worse than trash. Nevermind the decorated service history. Nevermind the bronze star. The commendations. The honorable discharge. Nor the trauma from 2 deployments. From carrying broken and bleeding civies under fire. From carrying my brothers. From attending their funerals. None of that matters to any of them anymore. So none of them matter to me anymore. My friends and family love me for me, accept me for me. We booked our tickets and made our plans to escape the coming dystopian nightmare that the US will turn into. Headed to the EU. Hi, I'm BananasRepublic and I am a woman. I'm proud to be me. If you're out there and scared, I'm proud of you too. If you're thinking of going into the closet to be safe during these "interesting times" know I don't think ANY less of you. Trans MTF, Trans FTM, trans masc, trans fem, I'm so fucking proud of all of you. I want you to read those words again. I'm proud of you. To take the chance to be the real you, not the you anyone else wants you to be, takes courage. Know there are options to escape. I'm leaving the country, yes, but I fully plan on trying to help others escape if possible too before our very own Kristallnacht happens. Stay strong. Attempt to keep calm. And UwU on. Actually no UwU-ing, right to jail. (Psst or UwU it's fine fuck the police)
item.dc:creator: BananasRepublic

item.title: Cake day mini dump
item.description:
Well, I guess my cake day was actually yesterday, as it's now 5 min after midnight, but I still wanted to post because it's the 1st one I've remembered in my 8 years as an imgurian! (Boy, that was a long sentence. Don't come at me grammar police!) Help me celebrate with upvotes instead!
(I'm a woman who loves a nice grey sweatpant.)
item.dc:creator: justyouraveragepeacock

item.title: Dogs and Pizza
item.description:
Hi everyone, I'm MrPuckett I like fostering reactive or high-anxiety dogs that are seemingly impossible to adopt.
A couple weeks of training and long runs with my dogs can work wonders on fosters like Lilo and open her chances of finding a home. Above was her on day one and she was terrified of me Below is her on day 8 after 42 miles of running with my dogs -
It's a whole new Lilo! Running not only helps them calm down in the home but it also helps them bond with me and my dogs. It exposes them to all the scary sights and sounds of the city to show them there's nothing to be afraid of.
Krystal was labeled as "dog aggressive" and she couldn't be adopted by any home that already has a dog The majority of our adopters already have a dog and Krystal had a much smaller chance of ever finding home So let's bring her in and see what happens!
Yeah, Krystal was not great with other dogs. So let's run her and train her and maybe she'll see that dogs are friends and not enemies -
She did great! After only a week we took the restrictions off her bio and Krystal was adopted by a family with another chihuahua She just needed a chance to thrive.
Snoop Dog was a tough one. Of the 11 fosters I've had the last 6 months he was the only one that actually tried to harm my dogs. Let's get him into shape, let's train him and let's give him all the opportunity in the world to bond with my dogs
It's a brand new Snoop Dog!!! The most cuddly and affection dog who now loves playing gently with PJ and roughhousing with MrsPuckett
He's just the happiest dog in the world it's amazing He just needed a chance to shine.
You might wonder "Abe aren't you afraid of your dogs getting attacked?!" But these 2 play just as important of a role as I do in turning these fosters' lives around
When we take in a new dog that's scared of it's own shadow or has aggression issues, it's important that they get all the space in the world. My dogs won't go anywhere near a new foster in the apartment until that dog is ready to socialize.
As that foster starts to settle down and open up, my dogs will interact with them a little more each day Running with 3 onleash dogs can be pretty tough but the only thing the foster has to do is follow my dogs lead If my dogs aren't terrified then neither is the foster!
PJ especially has come a really long way, he was so scared of the big dogs when we first started crisis fostering last year Now he understands that every dog that walks in the house needs his help, they need him to be a role model.
Today is the first day I've really sat down and looked at all of these before and after pictures It's amazing what a little guidance and a lot of miles can do
Like for Calvin, another "dog-aggressive" dog that in only a week went from Unadoptable to great with my dogs to available for adoption to actually getting adopted!
It really goes to show you that dogs, just like humans - aren't broken forever. Sometimes we all need an environment change and someone who loves us despite our faults.
I also like to use these posts as a reminder to everyone of TheFreePizzaDude Sometimes it can feel like interest in that is fading and I want to do everything in my power to make sure it remains a helpful service to everyone
It's one of the most important things in the world to me after so many people sent me food when I was homeless years ago So if you're around Saturday afternoon stop by @TheFreePizzaDude post if you're able to send a meal to someone in need You can always contribute to the Imgurian Meal Fund and we've struggled quite a bit with that lately.
In the same way that Lilo was never fully broken, the same way that Snoop and Cobbler and Calvin and all these dogs aren't broken forever Neither are we. These dogs can't ask for a helping hand but every single one of us can
So whether you need food, or mental health help or just reassurance that the world isn't going to end tonight If you need a hand, reach out. If you don't, then grab ahold of someone reaching. Even if it's an 8lb chihuahua with a growl. ... TheFreePizzaDude 2pm central Saturday! If you can't make it or would like to help us keep The Imgurian Meal Fund alive you can do that below https://www.paypal.me/TheFreePizzaDude https://ko-fi.com/thefreepizzadude ... Thank you for reading my post, stay warm out there
item.dc:creator: MrPuckett

item.title: Dammit Chad
item.description: Message to my fellow straight dudes, always go down on a woman before she goes down on you. It’ll… change things in your life.

item.title: Good friends will keep you clean. :)
item.dc:creator: Luvlyquants

item.title: The struggle bus is real.
item.description: I wish I could say that this is seasonal, but I've been battling with severe depression for years. But I finally hit rock bottom again, and I'm struggling to maintain my composure. You'd figure the beautiful wife, big house, and nice car would ease the pain, but it doesn't. However, I am proud to say that I didn't pull the trigger two months ago nor follow through with suffocating myself last month. I just simply remind myself that this pain is temporary, and I just need to wait it out. If anyone wants to chat or is curious about anything, don't be afraid to ask.
item.dc:creator: HannibalLectersCatMittens

This is SO him
Oops (I was obsessed with him long before we started dating)
My sternum cracks all the time
Men’s whore shorts >>
Newsies
These are lesbians but it made me cry so I had to send it to him
Us as cloned gay dogs
Liza Jane and Froggy tax
Scruffy and Nessie tax. I love you @chriscaprisun
